title: Validate scheduler package split and full test suite
description: Run targeted and full-suite validation for the package split, checking import compatibility, module/test size constraints, and end-to-end test parity after the scheduler and test decompositions land.
acceptance_criteria: `from agent_takt.scheduler import Scheduler`, `SchedulerReporter`, and `SchedulerResult` works unchanged.
Scheduler package modules are each at or below 500 lines, and scheduler test files are each at or below 600 lines.
`uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q` passes after the split.
Any regressions introduced by the refactor are either fixed or reported precisely.

handoff_summary.completed: 1. Verified `from agent_takt.scheduler import Scheduler, SchedulerReporter, SchedulerResult` resolves correctly. 2. Verified all scheduler source modules are ≤500 lines (max: followups.py at 492). 3. Ran targeted test suite across the three known-failing files; confirmed 26 failures still present and unchanged from prior run.
handoff_summary.remaining: 26 failing tests need developer fixes: (1) test_scheduler_followups.py (7 tests): add forwarding methods or expose `_executor._followups` delegation for `_populate_shared_followup_touched_files`, `_planner_owned_followup`, `_existing_followups_for` on Scheduler; (2) test_config_wiring_phase3.py (14 tests): update `_read_scheduler_source` to read `scheduler/__init__.py` or concatenate package files, expose `lease_timeout_minutes` on Scheduler, fix `_create_corrective_bead`/`_create_followups` call paths; (3) test_model_override.py (5 tests): fix `_create_followups` calls to use new delegation path.
handoff_summary.risks: All 26 failures are in test files that directly access private Scheduler methods relocated to sub-managers. These are not regressions in the production scheduler behavior — only test access paths are broken. The scheduler's public behavior (run_once, scheduling, followups, correctives) remains fully covered by tests/test_orchestrator.py and the new split test files which all pass.
handoff_summary.next_action: Developer bead must update test_scheduler_followups.py, test_config_wiring_phase3.py, and test_model_override.py to use the new delegation paths. Specifically: (1) add forwarding properties/methods on Scheduler for the 3 FollowupManager methods tested directly; or update tests to access via scheduler._executor._followups; (2) fix _read_scheduler_source() to read from the scheduler package path; (3) expose lease_timeout_minutes on Scheduler; (4) fix _create_followups/_create_corrective_bead access in tests.
handoff_summary.block_reason: 26 failing tests: 7 in test_scheduler_followups.py (FollowupManager methods not forwarded through Scheduler), 14 in test_config_wiring_phase3.py (scheduler.py path hardcoded + lease_timeout_minutes missing + relocated method calls), 5 in test_model_override.py (_create_followups relocated). Full targeted run: 26 failed, 76 passed.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: test_scheduler_followups.py, test_config_wiring_phase3.py, and test_model_override.py all need developer updates. No source file changes required for the fix.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Line count constraint (AC #2, 600-line limit for test files) is exceeded by test_scheduler_followups.py (1165 lines) and test_scheduler_beads.py (791 lines). This is acknowledged as acceptable precedent per the bead's known_limitations field — these files cannot be split further without artificial fragmentation. Not blocking.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: AC #1 (public imports): PASS. AC #2 (source module line counts ≤500): PASS (max 492 for followups.py). AC #2 (test file line counts ≤600): FAIL for test_scheduler_followups.py (1165) and test_scheduler_beads.py (791) — acknowledged acceptable in known_limitations. AC #3 (full suite passes): FAIL — 26 regressions. AC #4 (regressions reported): DONE — all 26 failures precisely identified with root cause.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Line count constraint exceeded by test_scheduler_followups.py (1165) and test_scheduler_beads.py (791) — acceptable per bead's known_limitations. The 26 failures are in test access paths only; underlying production scheduler behavior is not regressed.

conflict_risks: test_scheduler_followups.py, test_config_wiring_phase3.py, and test_model_override.py all need developer updates. No source file changes required for the fix.

execution_history.summary: 26 test failures detected across 3 files. All are regressions from the scheduler package split: private methods (`_populate_shared_followup_touched_files`, `_planner_owned_followup`, `_existing_followups_for`, `_create_followups`, `_create_corrective_bead`) were moved from `Scheduler` to `FollowupManager`/`BeadExecutor` sub-managers, but the three existing test files (`test_scheduler_followups.py`, `test_config_wiring_phase3.py`, `test_model_override.py`) were not updated to use the new delegation paths. Additionally, `test_config_wiring_phase3.py` hard-codes the path `src/agent_takt/scheduler.py` which no longer exists (it is now a package directory). Public API imports (`from agent_takt.scheduler import Scheduler, SchedulerReporter, SchedulerResult`) work correctly. All scheduler package source modules are ≤500 lines. Two test files exceed the 600-line AC limit (test_scheduler_followups.py: 1165, test_scheduler_beads.py: 791) but this is acknowledged in known_limitations as acceptable precedent.
Created corrective bead B-09c5359d-corrective for blocked issue
Requeued blocked bead after transient infrastructure/auth error

Ran targeted validation for the scheduler package split. Public API imports pass, all scheduler source modules are ≤500 lines. 26 test failures remain in test_scheduler_followups.py (7), test_config_wiring_phase3.py (14), and test_model_override.py (5) — all caused by private methods relocated from Scheduler to sub-managers during the package split that were not forwarded through the public Scheduler interface. Developer fixes required.
Requeued blocked bead after transient infrastructure/auth error

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

**CRITICAL — read before any tool use:**
- **Never use `run_in_background: true`** on any Bash command. All test commands must run synchronously so their output is captured before the structured verdict is emitted. Background tasks cause the structured output to be lost, resulting in a failed bead.
- **If a task-notification appears in your context**, that means a previous Bash command ran in the background in violation of this rule. Do not treat the notification as a sign that work is done. Re-run the test command synchronously (without `run_in_background`) and emit the JSON verdict block as your final response.
- **The JSON verdict block must be the absolute last output of your response.** Never end with prose, a status summary, or a notification acknowledgement. The orchestrator parses your final message as JSON and will fail if it is not valid JSON.

---

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run only the test files related to the bead's changed files, not the full test suite. Target individual test files or modules. If unsure which tests are relevant, use the bead's expected_files and touched_files fields as a guide. Defer command mechanics (how to run, which runner, which flags) to `.agents/skills/capability/test-execution/SKILL.md`.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes (e.g. import corrections, fixture setup) only when strictly necessary to run the relevant tests. Do not refactor production code under the guise of test enablement.

Disallowed actions:
- Run the full test suite instead of targeting specific tests. Always run targeted, scoped commands. Running the full suite wastes time and often exceeds the agent timeout.
- Use `run_in_background` for any Bash commands. (See CRITICAL section above.)
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the tester signoff decision: `approved` means testing can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when testing is complete with no unresolved tester-scope findings.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved defect or coverage gap count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when follow-up work is required.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.

**FINAL REMINDER: The JSON verdict block must be the last thing you output. Do not add any text, prose, or acknowledgement after it.**

acceptance_criteria: `from agent_takt.scheduler import Scheduler`, `SchedulerReporter`, and `SchedulerResult` works unchanged.

handoff_summary.completed: 1. Verified public API imports resolve correctly via uv run. 2. Verified all scheduler submodule imports work without circular imports. 3. Verified all scheduler source modules are ≤500 lines (max: followups.py at 492). 4. Ran full test suite; 956 passed, 26 failed.
handoff_summary.remaining: 26 failing tests need developer fixes: (1) test_scheduler_followups.py (7 tests): update `scheduler._populate_shared_followup_touched_files()`, `scheduler._planner_owned_followup()`, `scheduler._existing_followups_for()` calls to use `scheduler._executor._followups.*` delegation path; (2) test_config_wiring_phase3.py (14 tests): fix `scheduler.lease_timeout_minutes` access, `scheduler._create_corrective_bead()`, `scheduler._create_followups()` calls, and update FileNotFoundError tests that look for `scheduler.py` to look for `scheduler/__init__.py`; (3) test_model_override.py (5 tests): fix `scheduler._create_followups()` calls.

The FileNotFoundError tests in test_config_wiring_phase3.py check source file size and will need to reference the new package path.

Also fix FileNotFoundError tests that hardcode the old scheduler.py path.
handoff_summary.block_reason: 26 failing tests: 7 in test_scheduler_followups.py (methods moved to FollowupManager not forwarded through Scheduler), 14 in test_config_wiring_phase3.py (same + scheduler.py path hardcoded + lease_timeout_minutes missing), 5 in test_model_override.py (_create_followups relocated). Full suite: 26 failed, 956 passed.

handoff_summary.design_decisions: The three test files were not in scope of any completed bead and were not updated when methods migrated from Scheduler to sub-managers during the package split. A corrective developer bead should fix only the test files — no source changes needed.

AC #2 (test file line counts ≤600): FAIL for test_scheduler_followups.py (1165) and test_scheduler_beads.py (791) — acknowledged as acceptable in known_limitations.
